questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: When Albert Einstein shook hands with Max Planck, what type of force did they exert on each other?
questions.solution:
When two objects come into contact, the force exerted between them is a contact force, such as normal force or friction.

At the microscopic level, these contact forces arise due to the electromagnetic interactions between the electrons and protons of the atoms making up the interacting surfaces.

Gravitational force between the hands is negligible due to their small masses.

Strong nuclear and weak forces are short-range forces that operate only within the atomic nucleus and do not play a role in macroscopic contact.

Therefore, the force exerted during a handshake is fundamentally electromagnetic in nature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: The sum of all electromagnetic forces between different particles within a system of charged particles is zero
options: only if half of the particles are positively charged and the other half are negatively charged
only if all the particles are negatively charged
irrespective of the signs of the charges
only if all the particles are positively charged
questions.solution:
According to Newton's third law of motion, the electromagnetic force exerted by one particle on another is equal in magnitude and opposite in direction to the force exerted by the second particle on the first.

Mathematically, $\vec{F}_{ij} = -\vec{F}_{ji}$, where $\vec{F}_{ij}$ is the force on particle $i$ due to particle $j$.

When calculating the sum of all internal electromagnetic forces within a system of charged particles, these forces always occur in action-reaction pairs. The sum of all such pairs is exactly zero, since $\vec{F}_{ij} + \vec{F}_{ji} = 0$.

This cancellation depends only on the mutual interaction between the particles and holds true irrespective of the magnitudes or the signs of the charges involved.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: Suppose $E$, $G$, and $N$ denote the magnitudes of the electromagnetic, gravitational, and nuclear forces between two electrons at a specific separation. Which of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
Electrons are leptons and do not participate in strong nuclear interactions. Therefore, the magnitude of the nuclear force between two electrons is zero, i.e., $N = 0$.

The electromagnetic force $E$ between two electrons is much greater than the gravitational force $G$ between them. For two electrons, the ratio of the electromagnetic force to the gravitational force is approximately $10^{42}$, which means $E > G$.

Since $G > 0$ and $N = 0$, the correct order of the magnitudes of the forces is $E > G > N$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.q: If a $60 \text{ kg}$ man pushes a $40 \text{ kg}$ man with a force of $60 \text{ N}$, the force exerted by the $40 \text{ kg}$ man on the other man is
questions.solution:
According to Newton's third law of motion, to every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.

The force exerted by the $60 \text{ kg}$ man on the $40 \text{ kg}$ man is $60 \text{ N}$.

Therefore, the $40 \text{ kg}$ man exerts an equal and opposite force on the $60 \text{ kg}$ man.

The magnitude of the force exerted by the $40 \text{ kg}$ man is $60 \text{ N}$.
